feature.name: Respect overscroll-behavior for keyboard scrolls
feature.creator: flackr@chromium.org
feature.summary: When you set `overscroll-behavior` to a value other than `auto`, the browser should not perform scroll chaining. We respect this for mouse or touch scrolling, however keyboard scrolls were ignoring it. This change makes keyboard scrolling respect overscroll-behavior as well.

intake.finding: ChromeStatus record (created 2025-11-11 by flackr@chromium.org). When you set `overscroll-behavior` to a value other than `auto`, the browser should not perform scroll chaining. We respect this for mouse or touch scrolling, however keyboard scrolls were ignoring it. This change makes keyboard scrolling respect overscroll-behavior as well. Category: CSS. Blink component: Blink>Scroll. Stars: 72.
intake.limitations: ChromeStatus record fields only; intent thread content not directly accessible.

incubation.finding: Feature targets web feature 'overscroll-behavior'. This is an official WebDX feature. Problem definition depends on the spec/explainer which was not directly accessible.
incubation.limitations: No public explainer or incubation discussion accessible.

adoption.finding: The CSS property counter 'overscroll-behavior' (ChromeStatus timeline, bucket_id 585) measured 19.3518% of measured Chrome HTTP/HTTPS page loads on 2026-07-20. Tracked since 2017-08-19 with 3091 data points. Mapping confidence: name-matched-family.
adoption.limitations: Counter measures the overscroll-behavior family/property, not this specific feature. Does not isolate the Chrome 144 increment.

deprecation.finding: This is a capability addition or behavior change, not a deprecation.
